ground_truth_facts.fact: User wants to increase vertical jump by 5 inches for basketball performance
User has a state-level basketball tournament in approximately 6 weeks
User has a recurring mild right ankle sprain that flares up under heavy load
User follows intermittent fasting 16:8 and trains twice daily during tournament preparation
User has been feeling burnt out and signs of overtraining for the past two weeks
User was selected for the state basketball team for the first time this season

conversation_messages: Just got selected for the state basketball team — first time ever. Tournament is in 6 weeks. I need to peak at the right time.
My main goal physically is to add 5 inches to my vertical. My current standing jump is pretty average for my position.
I do intermittent fasting — 16:8 — have been for about a year. Works well for body composition.
Right now I'm doing two-a-days. Morning is skill work and conditioning, evening is weights. It's a lot.
My right ankle has this annoying history — it keeps spraining. It's mild each time but it keeps coming back, especially under heavy plyometric load.
Honestly I've been feeling really drained the last 2 weeks. Every session feels harder than it should. I dread waking up for morning practice.
My coach wants me to keep pushing because the tournament is close, but my body is telling me something different.
Is it okay to do heavy plyometrics on an empty stomach since I fast in the mornings?
I hit a new vertical jump PR last month before the fatigue kicked in. Jumped 28 inches. Want to get to 33.
My ankle swelled up slightly after yesterday's sprint drills. Should I be worried?
How do I manage training load in the last 2 weeks before the tournament without losing fitness?
I've been sleeping poorly too — maybe 5-6 hours. Is that making the fatigue worse?
What foods should I focus on during the eating window if I'm doing two-a-days on intermittent fasting?

probe_queries.query: How do I protect my ankle during intense training?
Is intermittent fasting good when I'm training twice a day?
I feel exhausted all the time, am I overtraining?
What plyometric exercises should I do to jump higher?
How should I taper my training before the tournament?
Should I train hard today or take a rest day?
